mission.title: Install and Configure Cedar on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cedar is a language for writing and enforcing authorization policies in applications. It allows you to specify fine-grained permissions, enabling your applications to authorize access requests efficiently and securely.

steps.title: Clone the Cedar repository
steps.description:
Clone the Cedar repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/cedar-policy/cedar.git
cd cedar
```

steps.title: Build the Cedar project
steps.description:
Build the Cedar project using Cargo, the Rust package manager:
```bash
cargo build --release
```

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes deployment for Cedar
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes deployment using the following manifest. Save it as `cedar-deployment.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: cedar
  namespace: cedar
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cedar
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: cedar
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cedar
        image: your-docker-registry/cedar:4.9.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
```
Then apply the manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f cedar-deployment.yaml --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Expose Cedar service
steps.description:
Expose the Cedar deployment as a service:
```bash
kubectl expose deployment cedar --type=LoadBalancer --name=cedar-service --namespace=cedar
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cedar pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace=cedar
```
You should see a pod with the name `cedar-<hash>` in the Running state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Cedar deployment by editing the `cedar-deployment.yaml` file to include resource limits:
```yaml
resources:
  requests:
    memory: "128Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  limits:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "1"
```
Then reapply the manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f cedar-deployment.yaml --namespace=cedar
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Cedar service
uninstall.description:
Remove the Cedar service with the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete service cedar-service --namespace=cedar
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Cedar deployment
uninstall.description:
Remove the Cedar deployment using:
```bash
kubectl delete deployment cedar --namespace=cedar
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Cedar namespace
uninstall.description:
Clean up by deleting the Cedar namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cedar
```

upgrade.title: Backup current Cedar deployment
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of the Cedar deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment cedar --namespace=cedar -o yaml > cedar-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Cedar image version
upgrade.description: Edit the `cedar-deployment.yaml` file to update the image version to the latest (e.g., `your-docker-registry/cedar:4.9.1`).

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Cedar deployment
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated deployment manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f cedar-deployment.yaml --namespace=cedar
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Cedar pod after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace=cedar
```
Ensure the pod is running with the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Cedar pod is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the Cedar pod to diagnose the issue:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace=cedar
```
Common issues may include incorrect image name or insufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
Verify if the service is correctly set up:
```bash
kubectl get services --namespace=cedar
```
Ensure the service type is LoadBalancer and check the external IP.

troubleshooting.title: Cedar deployment is crashing
troubleshooting.description:
Check the status of the pods:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace=cedar
```
Look for events indicating crashes or restarts.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Cedar pod running in the `cedar` namespace, and the service will be accessible. You can verify the deployment and service status using the provided commands.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: cedar
  namespace: cedar
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cedar
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: cedar
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cedar
        image: your-docker-registry/cedar:4.9.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed.
